items.headline: Jackson LaCombe helping Anaheim Ducks limit Connor McDavid in first round
items.description:

items.headline: Artturi Lehknonen ‘special player in playoffs’ for Colorado Avalanche
items.description: LOS ANGELES – Artturi Lehkonen came up ice with a head of steam with Logan O'Connor on a 2-on-1 short-handed opportunity. He said he meant to pass it to O’Connor, but the puck deflected off Adrian Kempe’s skate and went in the net instead for Lehkonen’s first career Stanley Cup

items.headline: Edmonton Oilers have not decided on Game 4 starting goalie
items.description:
ANAHEIM -- The Edmonton Oilers are contemplating a goalie change for Game 4 of the Western Conference First Round against the Anaheim Ducks at Honda Center on Sunday (9:30 p.m. ET; Victory+, ESPN, TVAS2, SN, CBC).
Tristan Jarry occupied the starter’s net closest to the Edmonton bench at practice Saturday,

items.headline: Carolina Hurricanes Philadelphia Flyers Pittsburgh Penguins 2026 playoff lookahead
items.description:
The Carolina Hurricanes will play the Philadelphia Flyers or the Pittsburgh Penguins in the Eastern Conference Second Round.
The Hurricanes advanced by defeating the Ottawa Senators 4-2 in Game 4 at Canadian Tire Centre on Saturday to sweep the best-of-7 series.

items.headline: Los Angeles Kings Anze Kopitar hoping to extend career, season in Game 4
items.description: EL SEGUNDO, Calif. -- Anze Kopitar understands his 20-year NHL career could conclude on Sunday.

items.headline: Carolina Hurricanes Ottawa Senators Game 4 recap April 25, 2026
items.description: OTTAWA -- Logan Stankoven scored the go-ahead goal in the third period, and the Carolina Hurricanes completed a series sweep of the Ottawa Senators with a 4-2 win in Game 4 of the Eastern Conference First Round at Canadian Tire Centre on Saturday.

items.headline: Carolina Hurricanes Nikolaj Ehlers injury status update April 25, 2026
items.description:
OTTAWA -- Nikolaj Ehlers did not play for the Carolina Hurricanes in their 4-2 win against the Ottawa Senators at Canadian Tire Centre on Saturday because of a lower-body injury. The Hurricanes eliminated the Senators with the Game 4 win to sweep the series.

items.headline: Martin St. Louis Montreal Canadiens Seinfeld comfort TV show
items.description:
Now here’s a story about nothing.
Montreal Canadiens head coach Martin St. Louis revealed Saturday that his comfort TV show is the iconic 90s sitcom, “Seinfeld.”
“To me, ‘Seinfeld’ is the show that can let me disconnect from everything,” St. Louis told reporters.
“It’s a show about nothing,” he added

items.description:

items.description:

items.headline: Tampa Bay Lightning defense stepping up without Victor Hedman in first round
items.description:
MONTREAL -- The Tampa Bay Lightning are doing their best to handle the absence of Victor Hedman, but competing without their top defenseman during the Stanley Cup Playoffs has added a significant challenge.

items.headline: Montreal not worried about top line entering Game 4 of NHL playoffs against Tampa Bay
items.description:
